message:
refactor(commands): extract /login and /logout handlers; improve provider "did you mean?" with Levenshtein

Two changes in this commit:

1. commands.rs: Extract the /login and /logout slash command handlers
   from interactive.rs inline dispatch into dedicated methods in the
   commands submodule. The /login handler (~150 lines) orchestrates the
   full OAuth/API-key flow: listing providers when no args given,
   normalizing provider input, initiating OAuth browser flow or API key
   prompt, and spawning async credential exchange. The /logout handler
   (~20 lines) removes stored credentials and confirms the action.
   This continues the interactive.rs decomposition.

2. providers/mod.rs: Replace the naive substring-based
   suggest_similar_providers() with a tiered matching system that includes
   Levenshtein edit distance for typo correction:
   - Tier 0: exact prefix match (highest confidence)
   - Tier 1: substring containment
   - Tier 2: edit distance within a length-scaled threshold
     (0 for 1-2 chars, 1 for 3-5, 2 for 6-9, 3 for 10+)
   The edit_distance() implementation uses a single-row O(min(a,b))
   buffer. max_edit_distance() scales the threshold to avoid false
   positives on very short inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
